articles.headline: Major Terror Attack in Pahalgam, J&K Claims 26 Lives; TRF Claims Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• At least 26 people, primarily tourists, were killed in a brutal terror attack in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir.
• The Resistance Front (TRF), an offshoot of the Pakistan-based, UN-designated terrorist group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), claimed responsibility for the coordinated assault.
• The attack has sparked widespread condemnation and raised serious concerns about the evolving security situation and cross-border terrorism in the region.
• Security forces have launched a massive cordon and search operation to apprehend the perpetrators and dismantle the terror network responsible.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenges of internal security and the need for robust counter-terrorism strategies in sensitive areas like Jammu and Kashmir.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Pahalgam is a popular tourist destination in Anantnag district of Jammu & Kashmir.
The Resistance Front (TRF) is an offshoot of Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), a UN-designated terrorist organization.
Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) is based in Pakistan and has been involved in numerous terror attacks in India.
Jammu and Kashmir continues to be a region affected by terrorism, despite efforts to restore normalcy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of terrorism in Jammu and Kashmir, including the emergence of new outfits like TRF.
Discuss the role of external state and non-state actors in perpetuating terrorism in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current counter-terrorism strategies and suggest measures for enhancing internal security in border regions.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such terror incidents on tourism and local livelihoods in J&K.
Debate the challenges of intelligence gathering and coordination in preventing terror attacks.
articles.imageDescription: Security forces conducting operations in a mountainous region of Jammu and Kashmir following a terror attack.
articles.trivia: Pahalgam is often referred to as the 'Valley of Shepherds' and serves as a base camp for the Amarnath Yatra.

articles.headline: India Pledges Renewed Commitment to Climate Action and Biodiversity on Earth Day 2025
articles.explainer:
• On Earth Day 2025, India reiterated its commitment to global climate action and biodiversity conservation, emphasizing its 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) movement.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted national achievements in renewable energy capacity addition and afforestation efforts.
• Discussions focused on the urgent need for enhanced global cooperation to meet Paris Agreement targets and protect critical ecosystems.
• Experts called for a stronger policy push towards circular economy principles and sustainable consumption patterns across all sectors.
• Special events across the nation underscored public awareness campaigns on plastic pollution, water conservation, and sustainable living.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Earth Day is observed annually on April 22nd.
The theme for Earth Day 2025 focused on 'Planet vs. Plastics' or similar critical environmental issues.
India's 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) movement was launched by PM Modi.
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.
India is a signatory to the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and challenges in achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
Analyze the significance of the 'LiFE' movement in promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's policies and initiatives for biodiversity conservation and combating plastic pollution.
Examine the socio-economic implications of climate change for vulnerable communities in India and the adaptation strategies being implemented.
Discuss the importance of global cooperation and technology transfer for addressing climate change and environmental degradation.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized globe with green foliage and hands protecting it, symbolizing environmental conservation on Earth Day.
articles.trivia: The first Earth Day was celebrated on April 22, 1970, in the United States, mobilizing 20 million Americans.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs EC to Implement Transparency Measures for Political Funding
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a directive to the Election Commission (EC) to implement additional transparency measures regarding political funding.
• The ruling came in response to petitions seeking greater accountability and disclosure of financial contributions to political parties, building on previous judgments.
• The court emphasized the citizen's right to information regarding the sources of political funding for a healthy democracy.
• The EC is now mandated to develop a framework for real-time disclosure of donations above a certain threshold, potentially including donor details for non-electoral bond contributions.
• This move aims to curb the influence of black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body under Article 324.
The Supreme Court has previously played a significant role in electoral reforms (e.g., ADPR vs. Union of India case).
The Representation of People's Act, 1951, governs the conduct of elections in India.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2018, later struck down by the Supreme Court in 2024.
The right to information is a fundamental right derived from Article 19(1)(a).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of transparency in political funding for the health of a democratic system.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in promoting electoral reforms and upholding democratic principles in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Election Commission in ensuring free and fair elections, particularly concerning financial transparency.
Examine the arguments for and against anonymous political donations and their impact on accountability.
Suggest further reforms that could enhance transparency and reduce the influence of money power in Indian elections.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books in front of the Indian Supreme Court building, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
articles.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen (1950-1958).

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focuses on Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its bi-monthly review, maintaining the repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision was influenced by easing retail inflation, which has moved closer to the target range, and a cautious optimism about India's economic growth trajectory.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and the need for continued vigilance on food inflation and crude oil prices.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest this stance provides stability for businesses and consumers, encouraging investment and consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting in India is set at 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2%-6%).
The RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Withdrawal of accommodation refers to the RBI's stance of gradually reducing liquidity in the system.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current monetary policy stance of the RBI and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Discuss the impact of global economic developments (e.g., crude oil prices, geopolitical tensions) on India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with a graph showing economic trends in the background.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Weather Satellite INSAT-3DRS
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the INSAT-3DRS, a next-generation meteorological satellite, from Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard a Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV), the satellite is designed to provide enhanced weather forecasting, disaster warning, and atmospheric data.
• INSAT-3DRS features advanced instruments, including an Imager and a Sounder, for more precise observations of weather patterns and ocean surfaces.
• This launch significantly boosts India's capability in climate monitoring, agricultural advisories, and disaster management.
• The mission underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space applications for societal benefit.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's primary launch site is the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh.
GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) is used for launching heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO).
INSAT series satellites are primarily used for telecommunications, broadcasting, meteorology, and search and rescue operations.
A Geosynchronous Satellite is one that orbits the Earth at the same rate as the Earth's rotation, appearing stationary from the ground.
The Imager and Sounder are key instruments for meteorological observation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced meteorological satellites like INSAT-3DRS for India's disaster management and agricultural sectors.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its role in global space exploration and applications.
Examine how indigenous space capabilities contribute to national development and self-reliance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging space technology for climate change monitoring and mitigation.
Evaluate the societal benefits derived from ISRO's various satellite missions.
articles.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket lifting off from a launch pad, with the Indian flag visible.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Free and Open Indo-Pacific, Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening bilateral economic ties.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, emphasizing maritime security and regional stability.
• Discussions covered collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, defense manufacturing, and resilient supply chains.
• Japan expressed support for India's infrastructure development projects, including high-speed rail and smart cities.
• The dialogue also addressed global challenges such as climate change, counter-terrorism, and UN reforms, reflecting a convergence of strategic interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / Japan Times
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue), along with the USA and Australia.
Japan is a key partner in India's Act East Policy.
Both countries share a vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The Strategic Dialogue is a high-level forum for discussing bilateral and regional issues.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership for maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in strengthening resilient supply chains between the two nations in a globalized world.
Examine how India's 'Act East Policy' aligns with Japan's Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the role of bilateral dialogues in addressing global challenges like climate change and regional security concerns.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together in a formal setting, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
articles.trivia: India and Japan celebrated 70 years of diplomatic relations in 2022.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Progress Report Highlights Gains in Maternal and Child Health
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released its annual progress report for the National Health Mission (NHM), showcasing significant improvements in key health indicators.
• The report highlighted a sustained decline in Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) and Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) across several states.
• Increased access to institutional deliveries, immunization coverage, and primary healthcare services were cited as major contributors to these gains.
• Challenges remain in addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and ensuring equitable access to healthcare in remote areas.
• The government reiterated its commitment to strengthening health infrastructure and human resources under the Ayushman Bharat scheme.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).
MMR is defined as the number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births.
IMR is defined as the number of deaths of infants under one year of age per 1,000 live births.
Ayushman Bharat comprises two components: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).
ASHA workers are a crucial link in delivering primary healthcare services in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Analyze the successes and failures of the National Health Mission in achieving its objectives of improving health outcomes in India.
Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to maternal and child mortality in India and the government's strategies to address them.
Evaluate the role of Ayushman Bharat in strengthening India's healthcare infrastructure and achieving universal health coverage.
Examine the challenges in delivering equitable healthcare services, particularly in rural and remote areas, and propose solutions.
Discuss the importance of public health interventions and community participation in improving health indicators.
articles.imageDescription: A healthcare worker interacting with a mother and child in a rural health centre, symbolizing public health efforts.
articles.trivia: India aims to reduce its MMR to 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030, in line with the Sustainable Development Goals.

articles.headline: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Registers Significant Progress in Pilot Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced substantial progress in pilot projects under India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities are now operational or nearing completion, demonstrating viability for industrial applications.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to decarbonization goals.
• Policy incentives for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen adoption in sectors like refining, fertilizer, and steel are being streamlined.
• This progress is crucial for India's energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023.
The mission aims to achieve a production capacity of 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen per annum by 2030.
Electrolyzers are devices that split water into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity.
Green hydrogen has applications in various 'hard-to-abate' sectors like steel, cement, and heavy-duty transport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and incentives in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen to decarbonize various industrial sectors in India.
Discuss how India's Green Hydrogen Mission aligns with global efforts towards energy transition and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic industrial complex with wind turbines and solar panels, symbolizing green hydrogen production.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Digital Public Infrastructure Expansion Targets Rural Areas for Enhanced Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The government announced a significant push to expand India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to deeper rural areas, focusing on financial inclusion.
• Initiatives include increasing the reach of Aadhaar-enabled Payment Systems (AePS), UPI, and open networks for digital commerce (ONDC).
• The aim is to bridge the digital divide and empower rural populations with access to digital services, banking, and markets.
• Training programs for digital literacy and awareness campaigns are being intensified to ensure effective adoption.
• This expansion is expected to foster economic growth, streamline public service delivery, and enhance transparency at the grassroots level.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
India Stack is a set of APIs that allows governments, businesses, startups and developers to utilize a unique digital infrastructure to solve India’s problems.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the UIDAI.
UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is an initiative aimed at promoting open networks for all aspects of exchanging goods and services over digital or electronic networks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving financial inclusion and rural development.
Analyze the challenges in expanding digital services to remote and digitally illiterate populations in India.
Evaluate the role of initiatives like UPI and ONDC in democratizing access to financial services and e-commerce for small businesses and individuals.
Examine the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital data collection and usage in DPI.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of DPI across diverse socio-economic groups.
articles.imageDescription: A digital graphic depicting interconnected networks and mobile payment interfaces, symbolizing DPI.
articles.trivia: India recorded over 12 billion UPI transactions in March 2024, showcasing its massive adoption.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Integrated Approach for Sustainable Water Management
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on 'Water Management and Conservation' to both Houses, advocating for an integrated and holistic approach.
• The report highlighted critical issues such as groundwater depletion, inter-state water disputes, and inefficient irrigation practices.
• Key recommendations included promoting water-efficient technologies, incentivizing rainwater harvesting, and strengthening inter-state cooperation on river basin management.
• The committee also emphasized the need for public awareness campaigns and community participation in water conservation efforts.
• The findings are expected to inform future policy decisions aimed at ensuring water security for the nation amidst growing demand and climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees constituted from time to time.
Water is primarily a State subject in India.
The Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
Groundwater is a major source of irrigation and drinking water in India.
Inter-state water disputes are resolved through tribunals established under the Inter-State River Water Disputes Act, 1956.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges associated with water management in India, including groundwater depletion and inter-state water disputes.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee report and their potential impact on national water policy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives (e.g., Jal Jeevan Mission) in ensuring water security.
Examine the role of federalism in addressing water-related issues and propose mechanisms for better inter-state cooperation.
Discuss the importance of public participation and technological innovations in promoting sustainable water conservation practices.
articles.imageDescription: A parched agricultural field contrasting with a lush green area, symbolizing water scarcity and conservation.
articles.trivia: India accounts for 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's renewable water resources.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly shown positive momentum after recent rounds of talks.
• Both sides expressed optimism about resolving key sticking points related to market access, tariffs, and intellectual property rights.
• A comprehensive FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• India is keen to gain better access for its agricultural products, textiles, and services, while the EU seeks reduced tariffs on automobiles and wines.
• The agreement is seen as a crucial step in strengthening strategic ties between India and the EU amidst evolving global trade dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is one of the EU's largest trading partners.
A Free Trade Agreement (FTA) aims to eliminate or reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
Negotiations for the India-EU FTA began in 2007, paused, and resumed in 2022.
Key sectors of interest for India in the FTA include textiles, pharmaceuticals, and services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the strategic implications of closer economic ties between India and the EU in the context of global trade realignments.
Evaluate the key contentious issues in FTA negotiations, such as market access and intellectual property rights, and their impact on India's interests.
Examine how an FTA could impact various sectors of the Indian economy, including agriculture, manufacturing, and services.
Discuss the role of FTAs in enhancing India's geopolitical influence and diversifying its trade partnerships.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union waving side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral economic partnership.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Restoration of Ancient Temple Complex in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a major restoration project for a centuries-old temple complex in the Puri district of Odisha.
• The temple, dating back to the Kalinga architectural style, holds significant historical and cultural importance, but has suffered from neglect and environmental damage.
• The restoration aims to preserve the intricate carvings, structural integrity, and unique architectural features of the complex.
• Local communities and heritage enthusiasts have welcomed the initiative, highlighting its potential to boost cultural tourism and awareness.
• This project is part of ASI's broader efforts to conserve India's rich architectural heritage and promote cultural preservation.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture.
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage in India.
Kalinga architecture is a distinct style of Hindu temple architecture that originated in ancient Kalinga (present-day Odisha).
Famous examples of Kalinga architecture include the Lingaraja Temple and Konark Sun Temple.
Puri is famous for the Jagannath Temple, a major pilgrimage site.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving India's architectural heritage for cultural identity and tourism.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in conserving ancient monuments across the country.
Evaluate the role of local communities and public-private partnerships in heritage preservation efforts.
Examine the impact of climate change and environmental degradation on historical structures and the measures taken for their protection.
Discuss the potential of cultural tourism to contribute to local economies and promote awareness of India's past.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists working meticulously on the restoration of an ancient stone temple, with scaffolding around it.
articles.trivia: The Konark Sun Temple in Odisha is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is designed in the shape of a colossal chariot.

articles.headline: Experts Discuss India's Urban Planning Challenges and Sustainable Solutions at National Conference
articles.explainer:
• A national conference on urban development brought together policymakers, urban planners, and academics to discuss India's growing urban challenges.
• Key issues addressed included rapid urbanization, inadequate infrastructure, housing shortages, and environmental pollution in cities.
• Discussions focused on implementing smart city principles, promoting green urban spaces, and developing resilient urban infrastructure.
• Experts emphasized the need for participatory planning, leveraging technology, and innovative financing models for sustainable urban growth.
• The conference concluded with recommendations for a revised National Urban Policy to address the complexities of India's urban future.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: India is experiencing rapid urbanization, with a significant portion of its population now living in urban areas.
The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
Housing for All by 2022 (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban) is a major government initiative.
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) are responsible for urban governance and service delivery.
Environmental pollution, including air and water pollution, is a major challenge in Indian cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by rapid urbanization in India, including infrastructure, housing, and environmental concerns.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like the Smart Cities Mission in addressing urban planning issues.
Evaluate the role of technology and innovative financing models in achieving sustainable urban development.
Examine the socio-economic implications of urban growth on different segments of society, particularly the urban poor.
Suggest a comprehensive approach for integrated urban planning that considers environmental, social, and economic sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: A panoramic view of a modern Indian city skyline with green spaces, symbolizing sustainable urban development.
articles.trivia: By 2050, India is projected to add 416 million urban dwellers, becoming the country with the largest urban population.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Amidst Changing Weather Patterns
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced enhanced support for climate-resilient agriculture initiatives across the country.
• The focus is on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and weather-based crop insurance schemes.
• Farmers are being encouraged to adopt sustainable farming practices, including agroforestry and organic farming, to mitigate climate change impacts.
• Funding for research and development into climate-smart agricultural technologies has been significantly increased.
• These measures aim to safeguard food security and farmer livelihoods in the face of increasingly unpredictable weather events and extreme climate conditions.

Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Land reforms in India
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare
prelimsPoints: India's agriculture sector is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts like droughts, floods, and extreme heat.
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is a crop insurance scheme for farmers.
Agroforestry involves integrating trees and shrubs into agricultural landscapes.
Precision irrigation techniques include drip and sprinkler irrigation, which conserve water.
Organic farming avoids the use of synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges posed by climate change to India's agricultural sector and its implications for food security.
Analyze the various government initiatives and policies aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of schemes like PMFBY and the adoption of sustainable farming practices by farmers.
Examine the role of research and technology in developing drought-resistant crops and improving agricultural productivity in changing climatic conditions.
Suggest a holistic strategy for ensuring farmer welfare and food security in the context of climate variability and extreme weather events.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field of drought-resistant crops under a clear sky, symbolizing agricultural resilience.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, cotton, and groundnuts.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Focuses on Digital Education and Skill Development for Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital education.
• Significant strides have been made in integrating technology into learning, including the development of digital content and online platforms.
• Emphasis is being placed on skill development and vocational training to enhance youth employability and align education with industry demands.
• Teacher training programs are being revamped to equip educators with the necessary skills for a digital-first and multidisciplinary learning environment.
• The policy aims to transform India into a knowledge society and global superpower by fostering holistic development and critical thinking among students.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
NEP 2020 proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) is a national platform for teachers and students.
The policy aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
Skill development is a core focus, aligning education with industry needs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and their potential to transform India's education system.
Analyze the role of digital education and technology integration in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020, especially in remote areas.
Evaluate the strategies adopted for promoting skill development and vocational training to address youth unemployment in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across diverse educational institutions and socio-economic contexts.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and foster a culture of critical thinking and innovation among students.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting using tablets and interactive screens, symbolizing digital education.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education in 1968.

prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for education

articles.headline: Enhanced Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Sector to Counter External Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated infrastructure development along India's eastern borders, particularly in Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim.
• Projects include the construction of strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to improve troop mobility and logistics.
• The initiative aims to bolster India's defense preparedness and ensure effective response to any external security challenges in the region.
• Modernization of border outposts and integration of new technologies are also key components of the strategy.
• This development is crucial for asserting sovereignty and safeguarding national interests in geopolitically sensitive border areas.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim share borders with China.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a premier road construction agency in India, developing and maintaining road networks in border areas.
India faces unresolved border disputes with China along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
Strategic infrastructure development is crucial for military logistics and rapid deployment in mountainous terrain.
The eastern sector is characterized by challenging geographical conditions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly in the eastern sector.
Analyze the challenges involved in infrastructure development in mountainous and remote border regions.
Evaluate the impact of such infrastructure projects on local communities and regional development.
Examine how enhanced border infrastructure contributes to India's defense preparedness and its ability to deter external aggression.
Discuss the geopolitical implications of India's border infrastructure push in the context of its relations with neighboring countries.
articles.imageDescription: Engineers and construction workers building a strategic road in a high-altitude, mountainous border region.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, located in the Pir Panjal range of the Himalayas, is the world's longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet.

articles.headline: India Remains Attractive FDI Destination Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• A new report on global Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) trends highlighted India's continued attractiveness as a preferred destination for foreign capital.
• Despite global economic headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties, India's robust domestic market, policy stability, and ease of doing business reforms contributed to sustained inflows.
• Key sectors attracting FDI included manufacturing, digital services, renewable energy, and infrastructure.
• The government reiterated its commitment to further liberalizing FDI norms and improving the investment climate to capitalize on global supply chain realignments.
• Sustained FDI inflows are crucial for employment generation, technology transfer, and overall economic growth in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Investment models
articles.source: UNCTAD / Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: FDI (Foreign Direct Investment) is an investment made by a firm or individual in one country into business interests located in another country.
India has implemented several reforms to improve its 'Ease of Doing Business' ranking.
The 'Make in India' initiative aims to boost domestic manufacturing and attract foreign investment.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes offer incentives to boost domestic manufacturing in various sectors.
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) is responsible for FDI policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors that make India an attractive destination for Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) despite global economic uncertainties.
Analyze the role of government policies and reforms (e.g., Make in India, PLI schemes) in boosting FDI inflows and industrial growth.
Evaluate the impact of FDI on employment generation, technology transfer, and overall economic development in India.
Examine the challenges faced by India in sustaining and increasing FDI, including infrastructure bottlenecks and regulatory hurdles.
Suggest further measures to enhance India's investment climate and capitalize on global supply chain realignments.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing upward trend arrows and currency symbols, representing FDI inflows into India.
articles.trivia: Mauritius and Singapore have historically been the top source countries for FDI in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign Direct Investment and Economic Reforms

articles.headline: New Wildlife Conservation Project Launched in Western Ghats to Protect Endemic Species
articles.explainer:
• A major new conservation project was launched in the Western Ghats region, focusing on the protection of its rich biodiversity and endemic species.
• The initiative, a collaboration between government agencies, NGOs, and local communities, aims to combat habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict.
• Efforts include habitat restoration, anti-poaching patrols, community engagement programs, and scientific research into endangered flora and fauna.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a global biodiversity hotspot facing increasing environmental pressures.
• This project is crucial for safeguarding the ecological integrity of the region and ensuring the survival of its unique wildlife.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and its conservation
Ecology and Ecosystems
articles.source: WWF-India / Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the eight hottest biodiversity hotspots in the world.
It is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Endemic species are those found exclusively in a particular geographical area.
Major threats to biodiversity in the Western Ghats include habitat fragmentation, deforestation, and climate change.
NGOs and local communities play a vital role in conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the multi-stakeholder approach adopted in the new conservation project and its potential for success.
Evaluate the challenges of human-wildlife conflict in densely populated regions and strategies for mitigation.
Examine the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in effective wildlife conservation.
Suggest policy measures and scientific interventions to enhance the protection of endemic species and maintain ecological balance in sensitive regions.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with diverse flora and fauna, symbolizing a biodiversity hotspot.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species of flora and fauna.

highlights: Pahalgam Terror Attack: 26 killed in J&K; TRF (LeT offshoot) claims responsibility, raising internal security concerns.
Earth Day 2025: India reaffirms climate action and biodiversity pledges, emphasizing 'LiFE' movement and sustainable development.
Supreme Court Directs EC: New transparency measures for political funding mandated to ensure democratic integrity.
RBI Monetary Policy: Repo rate maintained amidst easing inflation, signaling focus on balancing growth with price stability.
ISRO Launches INSAT-3DRS: Next-gen weather satellite enhances India's meteorological capabilities for forecasting and disaster warning.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding 'The Resistance Front' (TRF) is/are correct?
1. It is an offshoot of the Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba.
2. It primarily operates in the North-Eastern states of India.
3. It is a UN-designated terrorist group.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as TRF is an offshoot of Lashkar-e-Taiba. Statement 2 is incorrect as TRF primarily operates in Jammu and Kashmir. Statement 3 is correct as Lashkar-e-Taiba, and by extension its offshoots like TRF, are UN-designated terrorist groups.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary aim is to achieve a production capacity of 10 MMT of green hydrogen per annum by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
3. The mission seeks to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the mission aims for 5 MMT of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, not 10 MMT. Statement 2 is correct, as green hydrogen is defined by its production method using renewable energy for electrolysis. Statement 3 is correct, as India's mission envisions becoming a leader in green hydrogen production and export.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Health Mission (NHM), which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It was launched by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).
2. ASHA workers are a crucial link in delivering primary healthcare services under NHM.
3. It primarily focuses on reducing Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in urban areas.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; NHM was indeed launched by subsuming NRHM and NUHM. Statement 2 is correct, as ASHA workers are integral to primary healthcare delivery. Statement 3 is incorrect; while NHM addresses NCDs, its primary focus is broader, including maternal and child health, and it covers both rural and urban areas, not just NCDs in urban areas.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) To manage the government's fiscal deficit.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(c) To regulate foreign exchange rates exclusively.
(d) To promote exports and reduce imports.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is to maintain price stability, which is essential for sustainable growth. While other options relate to economic policy, they are not the central mandate of the MPC.

prelimsMocks.question:
The term 'India Stack' is often mentioned in the context of Digital Public Infrastructure. Which of the following components are generally considered part of the India Stack?
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN)
4. Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and ONDC are core components of the India Stack, representing identity, payments, and commerce layers, respectively. While GSTN is a crucial digital infrastructure for taxation, it is generally not categorized as part of the India Stack's foundational layers in the same way as the others.

mainsMocks.question: The recent terror attack in Pahalgam underscores the persistent challenges to India's internal security from cross-border terrorism. Analyze the evolving nature of these threats and critically evaluate the effectiveness of India's counter-terrorism strategies, suggesting measures for enhancement. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Pahalgam attack as a case study. Discuss the evolving nature of threats (hybrid terror, local recruitment, social media, foreign links). Evaluate current strategies (intelligence, border management, community engagement, legal framework). Suggest enhancements (tech integration, international cooperation, addressing radicalization, economic development).

mainsMocks.question: India's commitment to climate action and biodiversity conservation, as highlighted on Earth Day, is crucial for sustainable development. Discuss the key initiatives undertaken by India in this regard and analyze the challenges in achieving its environmental goals, particularly concerning climate change and pollution. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with India's pledges on Earth Day. Detail initiatives like LiFE, renewable energy targets, afforestation, national missions (Green Hydrogen). Discuss challenges like rapid industrialization, population pressure, funding gaps, technological limitations, and balancing development with conservation. Conclude with multi-faceted solutions.

mainsMocks.question: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) has emerged as a cornerstone of India's economic growth and financial inclusion efforts. Examine the transformative potential of DPI in empowering rural populations and discuss the associated challenges, including digital divide and data privacy concerns. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and its components (Aadhaar, UPI, ONDC). Explain its potential for financial inclusion (AePS, digital payments), access to services, and market access for rural areas. Discuss challenges: digital literacy, infrastructure (connectivity), data privacy, cybersecurity, and ensuring equitable access. Conclude with policy implications.

quickBytes.text: The Resistance Front (TRF) is an offshoot of Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), a UN-designated terrorist group.
Earth Day is observed annually on April 22nd, promoting environmental protection and sustainability.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI is a six-member body responsible for fixing India's benchmark interest rate.
INSAT-3DRS is a next-generation meteorological satellite launched by ISRO for enhanced weather forecasting.
India's 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) movement promotes sustainable living practices.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight hottest biodiversity hotspots and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture, responsible for heritage conservation.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, focusing on holistic and multidisciplinary education.
The Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through tap connections to all rural households by 2024.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Restoration of Ancient Temple Complex in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a major restoration project for a centuries-old temple complex in the Puri district of Odisha.
• The temple, dating back to the Kalinga architectural style, holds significant historical and cultural importance, but has suffered from neglect and environmental damage.
• The restoration aims to preserve the intricate carvings, structural integrity, and unique architectural features of the complex.
• Local communities and heritage enthusiasts have welcomed the initiative, highlighting its potential to boost cultural tourism and awareness.
• This project is part of ASI's broader efforts to conserve India's rich architectural heritage and promote cultural preservation.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture.
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage in India.
Kalinga architecture is a distinct style of Hindu temple architecture that originated in ancient Kalinga (present-day Odisha).
Famous examples of Kalinga architecture include the Lingaraja Temple and Konark Sun Temple.
Puri is famous for the Jagannath Temple, a major pilgrimage site.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving India's architectural heritage for cultural identity and tourism.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in conserving ancient monuments across the country.
Evaluate the role of local communities and public-private partnerships in heritage preservation efforts.
Examine the impact of climate change and environmental degradation on historical structures and the measures taken for their protection.
Discuss the potential of cultural tourism to contribute to local economies and promote awareness of India's past.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists working meticulously on the restoration of an ancient stone temple, with scaffolding around it.
GS1.trivia: The Konark Sun Temple in Odisha is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is designed in the shape of a colossal chariot.

GS1.headline: Experts Discuss India's Urban Planning Challenges and Sustainable Solutions at National Conference
GS1.explainer:
• A national conference on urban development brought together policymakers, urban planners, and academics to discuss India's growing urban challenges.
• Key issues addressed included rapid urbanization, inadequate infrastructure, housing shortages, and environmental pollution in cities.
• Discussions focused on implementing smart city principles, promoting green urban spaces, and developing resilient urban infrastructure.
• Experts emphasized the need for participatory planning, leveraging technology, and innovative financing models for sustainable urban growth.
• The conference concluded with recommendations for a revised National Urban Policy to address the complexities of India's urban future.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
GS1.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: India is experiencing rapid urbanization, with a significant portion of its population now living in urban areas.
The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
Housing for All by 2022 (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban) is a major government initiative.
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) are responsible for urban governance and service delivery.
Environmental pollution, including air and water pollution, is a major challenge in Indian cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by rapid urbanization in India, including infrastructure, housing, and environmental concerns.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like the Smart Cities Mission in addressing urban planning issues.
Evaluate the role of technology and innovative financing models in achieving sustainable urban development.
Examine the socio-economic implications of urban growth on different segments of society, particularly the urban poor.
Suggest a comprehensive approach for integrated urban planning that considers environmental, social, and economic sustainability.
GS1.imageDescription: A panoramic view of a modern Indian city skyline with green spaces, symbolizing sustainable urban development.
GS1.trivia: By 2050, India is projected to add 416 million urban dwellers, becoming the country with the largest urban population.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs EC to Implement Transparency Measures for Political Funding
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a directive to the Election Commission (EC) to implement additional transparency measures regarding political funding.
• The ruling came in response to petitions seeking greater accountability and disclosure of financial contributions to political parties, building on previous judgments.
• The court emphasized the citizen's right to information regarding the sources of political funding for a healthy democracy.
• The EC is now mandated to develop a framework for real-time disclosure of donations above a certain threshold, potentially including donor details for non-electoral bond contributions.
• This move aims to curb the influence of black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body under Article 324.
The Supreme Court has previously played a significant role in electoral reforms (e.g., ADPR vs. Union of India case).
The Representation of People's Act, 1951, governs the conduct of elections in India.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2018, later struck down by the Supreme Court in 2024.
The right to information is a fundamental right derived from Article 19(1)(a).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of transparency in political funding for the health of a democratic system.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in promoting electoral reforms and upholding democratic principles in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Election Commission in ensuring free and fair elections, particularly concerning financial transparency.
Examine the arguments for and against anonymous political donations and their impact on accountability.
Suggest further reforms that could enhance transparency and reduce the influence of money power in Indian elections.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books in front of the Indian Supreme Court building, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
GS2.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen (1950-1958).

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Free and Open Indo-Pacific, Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening bilateral economic ties.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, emphasizing maritime security and regional stability.
• Discussions covered collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, defense manufacturing, and resilient supply chains.
• Japan expressed support for India's infrastructure development projects, including high-speed rail and smart cities.
• The dialogue also addressed global challenges such as climate change, counter-terrorism, and UN reforms, reflecting a convergence of strategic interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / Japan Times
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue), along with the USA and Australia.
Japan is a key partner in India's Act East Policy.
Both countries share a vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The Strategic Dialogue is a high-level forum for discussing bilateral and regional issues.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership for maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in strengthening resilient supply chains between the two nations in a globalized world.
Examine how India's 'Act East Policy' aligns with Japan's Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the role of bilateral dialogues in addressing global challenges like climate change and regional security concerns.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together in a formal setting, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan celebrated 70 years of diplomatic relations in 2022.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Progress Report Highlights Gains in Maternal and Child Health
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released its annual progress report for the National Health Mission (NHM), showcasing significant improvements in key health indicators.
• The report highlighted a sustained decline in Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) and Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) across several states.
• Increased access to institutional deliveries, immunization coverage, and primary healthcare services were cited as major contributors to these gains.
• Challenges remain in addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and ensuring equitable access to healthcare in remote areas.
• The government reiterated its commitment to strengthening health infrastructure and human resources under the Ayushman Bharat scheme.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).
MMR is defined as the number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births.
IMR is defined as the number of deaths of infants under one year of age per 1,000 live births.
Ayushman Bharat comprises two components: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).
ASHA workers are a crucial link in delivering primary healthcare services in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Analyze the successes and failures of the National Health Mission in achieving its objectives of improving health outcomes in India.
Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to maternal and child mortality in India and the government's strategies to address them.
Evaluate the role of Ayushman Bharat in strengthening India's healthcare infrastructure and achieving universal health coverage.
Examine the challenges in delivering equitable healthcare services, particularly in rural and remote areas, and propose solutions.
Discuss the importance of public health interventions and community participation in improving health indicators.
GS2.imageDescription: A healthcare worker interacting with a mother and child in a rural health centre, symbolizing public health efforts.
GS2.trivia: India aims to reduce its MMR to 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030, in line with the Sustainable Development Goals.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Integrated Approach for Sustainable Water Management
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on 'Water Management and Conservation' to both Houses, advocating for an integrated and holistic approach.
• The report highlighted critical issues such as groundwater depletion, inter-state water disputes, and inefficient irrigation practices.
• Key recommendations included promoting water-efficient technologies, incentivizing rainwater harvesting, and strengthening inter-state cooperation on river basin management.
• The committee also emphasized the need for public awareness campaigns and community participation in water conservation efforts.
• The findings are expected to inform future policy decisions aimed at ensuring water security for the nation amidst growing demand and climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees constituted from time to time.
Water is primarily a State subject in India.
The Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
Groundwater is a major source of irrigation and drinking water in India.
Inter-state water disputes are resolved through tribunals established under the Inter-State River Water Disputes Act, 1956.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges associated with water management in India, including groundwater depletion and inter-state water disputes.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee report and their potential impact on national water policy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives (e.g., Jal Jeevan Mission) in ensuring water security.
Examine the role of federalism in addressing water-related issues and propose mechanisms for better inter-state cooperation.
Discuss the importance of public participation and technological innovations in promoting sustainable water conservation practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A parched agricultural field contrasting with a lush green area, symbolizing water scarcity and conservation.
GS2.trivia: India accounts for 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's renewable water resources.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly shown positive momentum after recent rounds of talks.
• Both sides expressed optimism about resolving key sticking points related to market access, tariffs, and intellectual property rights.
• A comprehensive FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• India is keen to gain better access for its agricultural products, textiles, and services, while the EU seeks reduced tariffs on automobiles and wines.
• The agreement is seen as a crucial step in strengthening strategic ties between India and the EU amidst evolving global trade dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is one of the EU's largest trading partners.
A Free Trade Agreement (FTA) aims to eliminate or reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
Negotiations for the India-EU FTA began in 2007, paused, and resumed in 2022.
Key sectors of interest for India in the FTA include textiles, pharmaceuticals, and services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the strategic implications of closer economic ties between India and the EU in the context of global trade realignments.
Evaluate the key contentious issues in FTA negotiations, such as market access and intellectual property rights, and their impact on India's interests.
Examine how an FTA could impact various sectors of the Indian economy, including agriculture, manufacturing, and services.
Discuss the role of FTAs in enhancing India's geopolitical influence and diversifying its trade partnerships.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union waving side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral economic partnership.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Focuses on Digital Education and Skill Development for Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital education.
• Significant strides have been made in integrating technology into learning, including the development of digital content and online platforms.
• Emphasis is being placed on skill development and vocational training to enhance youth employability and align education with industry demands.
• Teacher training programs are being revamped to equip educators with the necessary skills for a digital-first and multidisciplinary learning environment.
• The policy aims to transform India into a knowledge society and global superpower by fostering holistic development and critical thinking among students.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
NEP 2020 proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) is a national platform for teachers and students.
The policy aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
Skill development is a core focus, aligning education with industry needs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and their potential to transform India's education system.
Analyze the role of digital education and technology integration in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020, especially in remote areas.
Evaluate the strategies adopted for promoting skill development and vocational training to address youth unemployment in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across diverse educational institutions and socio-economic contexts.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and foster a culture of critical thinking and innovation among students.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting using tablets and interactive screens, symbolizing digital education.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education in 1968.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for education

GS3.headline: Major Terror Attack in Pahalgam, J&K Claims 26 Lives; TRF Claims Responsibility
GS3.explainer:
• At least 26 people, primarily tourists, were killed in a brutal terror attack in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir.
• The Resistance Front (TRF), an offshoot of the Pakistan-based, UN-designated terrorist group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), claimed responsibility for the coordinated assault.
• The attack has sparked widespread condemnation and raised serious concerns about the evolving security situation and cross-border terrorism in the region.
• Security forces have launched a massive cordon and search operation to apprehend the perpetrators and dismantle the terror network responsible.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenges of internal security and the need for robust counter-terrorism strategies in sensitive areas like Jammu and Kashmir.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Pahalgam is a popular tourist destination in Anantnag district of Jammu & Kashmir.
The Resistance Front (TRF) is an offshoot of Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), a UN-designated terrorist organization.
Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) is based in Pakistan and has been involved in numerous terror attacks in India.
Jammu and Kashmir continues to be a region affected by terrorism, despite efforts to restore normalcy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of terrorism in Jammu and Kashmir, including the emergence of new outfits like TRF.
Discuss the role of external state and non-state actors in perpetuating terrorism in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current counter-terrorism strategies and suggest measures for enhancing internal security in border regions.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such terror incidents on tourism and local livelihoods in J&K.
Debate the challenges of intelligence gathering and coordination in preventing terror attacks.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces conducting operations in a mountainous region of Jammu and Kashmir following a terror attack.
GS3.trivia: Pahalgam is often referred to as the 'Valley of Shepherds' and serves as a base camp for the Amarnath Yatra.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Renewed Commitment to Climate Action and Biodiversity on Earth Day 2025
GS3.explainer:
• On Earth Day 2025, India reiterated its commitment to global climate action and biodiversity conservation, emphasizing its 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) movement.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change highlighted national achievements in renewable energy capacity addition and afforestation efforts.
• Discussions focused on the urgent need for enhanced global cooperation to meet Paris Agreement targets and protect critical ecosystems.
• Experts called for a stronger policy push towards circular economy principles and sustainable consumption patterns across all sectors.
• Special events across the nation underscored public awareness campaigns on plastic pollution, water conservation, and sustainable living.
