GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focuses on Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its bi-monthly review, maintaining the repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision was influenced by easing retail inflation, which has moved closer to the target range, and a cautious optimism about India's economic growth trajectory.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and the need for continued vigilance on food inflation and crude oil prices.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest this stance provides stability for businesses and consumers, encouraging investment and consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting in India is set at 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2%-6%).
The RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Withdrawal of accommodation refers to the RBI's stance of gradually reducing liquidity in the system.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current monetary policy stance of the RBI and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Discuss the impact of global economic developments (e.g., crude oil prices, geopolitical tensions) on India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with a graph showing economic trends in the background.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Weather Satellite INSAT-3DRS
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the INSAT-3DRS, a next-generation meteorological satellite, from Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard a Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV), the satellite is designed to provide enhanced weather forecasting, disaster warning, and atmospheric data.
• INSAT-3DRS features advanced instruments, including an Imager and a Sounder, for more precise observations of weather patterns and ocean surfaces.
• This launch significantly boosts India's capability in climate monitoring, agricultural advisories, and disaster management.
• The mission underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space applications for societal benefit.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's primary launch site is the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh.
GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) is used for launching heavy communication and meteorological satellites into Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO).
INSAT series satellites are primarily used for telecommunications, broadcasting, meteorology, and search and rescue operations.
A Geosynchronous Satellite is one that orbits the Earth at the same rate as the Earth's rotation, appearing stationary from the ground.
The Imager and Sounder are key instruments for meteorological observation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced meteorological satellites like INSAT-3DRS for India's disaster management and agricultural sectors.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its role in global space exploration and applications.
Examine how indigenous space capabilities contribute to national development and self-reliance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging space technology for climate change monitoring and mitigation.

GS3.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket lifting off from a launch pad, with the Indian flag visible.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Registers Significant Progress in Pilot Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced substantial progress in pilot projects under India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities are now operational or nearing completion, demonstrating viability for industrial applications.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to decarbonization goals.
• Policy incentives for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen adoption in sectors like refining, fertilizer, and steel are being streamlined.
• This progress is crucial for India's energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and achieving net-zero emissions targets.

Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023.
The mission aims to achieve a production capacity of 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen per annum by 2030.
Electrolyzers are devices that split water into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity.
Green hydrogen has applications in various 'hard-to-abate' sectors like steel, cement, and heavy-duty transport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.

Examine the potential of green hydrogen to decarbonize various industrial sectors in India.
Discuss how India's Green Hydrogen Mission aligns with global efforts towards energy transition and sustainable development.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic industrial complex with wind turbines and solar panels, symbolizing green hydrogen production.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: Digital Public Infrastructure Expansion Targets Rural Areas for Enhanced Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced a significant push to expand India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to deeper rural areas, focusing on financial inclusion.
• Initiatives include increasing the reach of Aadhaar-enabled Payment Systems (AePS), UPI, and open networks for digital commerce (ONDC).
• The aim is to bridge the digital divide and empower rural populations with access to digital services, banking, and markets.
• Training programs for digital literacy and awareness campaigns are being intensified to ensure effective adoption.
• This expansion is expected to foster economic growth, streamline public service delivery, and enhance transparency at the grassroots level.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
India Stack is a set of APIs that allows governments, businesses, startups and developers to utilize a unique digital infrastructure to solve India’s problems.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the UIDAI.
UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is an initiative aimed at promoting open networks for all aspects of exchanging goods and services over digital or electronic networks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving financial inclusion and rural development.
Analyze the challenges in expanding digital services to remote and digitally illiterate populations in India.
Evaluate the role of initiatives like UPI and ONDC in democratizing access to financial services and e-commerce for small businesses and individuals.
Examine the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital data collection and usage in DPI.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of DPI across diverse socio-economic groups.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital graphic depicting interconnected networks and mobile payment interfaces, symbolizing DPI.
GS3.trivia: India recorded over 12 billion UPI transactions in March 2024, showcasing its massive adoption.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Amidst Changing Weather Patterns
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced enhanced support for climate-resilient agriculture initiatives across the country.
• The focus is on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and weather-based crop insurance schemes.
• Farmers are being encouraged to adopt sustainable farming practices, including agroforestry and organic farming, to mitigate climate change impacts.
• Funding for research and development into climate-smart agricultural technologies has been significantly increased.
• These measures aim to safeguard food security and farmer livelihoods in the face of increasingly unpredictable weather events and extreme climate conditions.

GS3.headline: Enhanced Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Sector to Counter External Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated infrastructure development along India's eastern borders, particularly in Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim.
• Projects include the construction of strategic roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to improve troop mobility and logistics.
• The initiative aims to bolster India's defense preparedness and ensure effective response to any external security challenges in the region.
• Modernization of border outposts and integration of new technologies are also key components of the strategy.
• This development is crucial for asserting sovereignty and safeguarding national interests in geopolitically sensitive border areas.

Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim share borders with China.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a premier road construction agency in India, developing and maintaining road networks in border areas.
India faces unresolved border disputes with China along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
Strategic infrastructure development is crucial for military logistics and rapid deployment in mountainous terrain.
The eastern sector is characterized by challenging geographical conditions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly in the eastern sector.

Evaluate the impact of such infrastructure projects on local communities and regional development.

GS3.headline: India Remains Attractive FDI Destination Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• A new report on global Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) trends highlighted India's continued attractiveness as a preferred destination for foreign capital.
• Despite global economic headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties, India's robust domestic market, policy stability, and ease of doing business reforms contributed to sustained inflows.
• Key sectors attracting FDI included manufacturing, digital services, renewable energy, and infrastructure.
• The government reiterated its commitment to further liberalizing FDI norms and improving the investment climate to capitalize on global supply chain realignments.
• Sustained FDI inflows are crucial for employment generation, technology transfer, and overall economic growth in India.

Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes offer incentives to boost domestic manufacturing in various sectors.
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) is responsible for FDI policy.

GS3.headline: New Wildlife Conservation Project Launched in Western Ghats to Protect Endemic Species
GS3.explainer:
• A major new conservation project was launched in the Western Ghats region, focusing on the protection of its rich biodiversity and endemic species.
• The initiative, a collaboration between government agencies, NGOs, and local communities, aims to combat habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict.
• Efforts include habitat restoration, anti-poaching patrols, community engagement programs, and scientific research into endangered flora and fauna.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a global biodiversity hotspot facing increasing environmental pressures.
• This project is crucial for safeguarding the ecological integrity of the region and ensuring the survival of its unique wildlife.

Biodiversity and its conservation

Major threats to biodiversity in the Western Ghats include habitat fragmentation, deforestation, and climate change.
NGOs and local communities play a vital role in conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the multi-stakeholder approach adopted in the new conservation project and its potential for success.
